points.text: The global DNA read, write, and edit market is projected to surge to $67.7 billion by 2030, driven by CRISPR advances and expanding clinical applications.
Market growth is fueled by rapid expansion in clinical applications and increased investment in CRISPR-based therapeutics.
Kraig Biocraft Laboratories is advancing a transgenic silkworm platform designed to produce recombinant spider silk at commercial scale.
The company plans to begin large-scale deployment of approximately one million proprietary spider-silk silkworm eggs with production starting in early spring.
Target output includes up to 10 metric tons of recombinant spider-silk cocoons per month across three Vietnam facilities.
Recombinant spider silk fibers have achieved tensile strengths up to 1.79 gigapascals in testing.
The material demonstrates elasticity above 38%, placing it in a class compared with structural steel on a strength-to-weight basis.
Kraig’s fibers overcome limitations regarding stability in wet conditions, enabling fabrics cleaned using conventional washing methods.
The company is expanding R&D capacity through Project Atlas to advance higher-performance recombinant spider silk materials.
Industry analysts note new materials often enter through apparel before moving into more demanding sectors like medical applications.
CRISPR Therapeutics priced $550 million aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2031 in a private offering to qualified institutional buyers.
The notes offering was upsized from the previously announced $350 million aggregate principal amount of notes.
CRISPR Therapeutics has achieved a historic milestone with the approval of CASGEVY for eligible patients with sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia.
Kraig Biocraft Laboratories delivered over 1.3 metric tons of spider silk cocoons in a single month, marking a 5X increase over the previous high.
Kraig Biocraft Laboratories successfully deployed more than 700,000 BAM-1 Alpha production hybrids to advance its scale-up plan.
Intellia Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on revolutionizing medicine leveraging CRISPR gene editing.
Intellia Therapeutics presented longer-term clinical data for Lonvoguran Ziclumeran for Hereditary Angioedema at AAAAI 2026.
CRISPR Therapeutics continues to expand its leadership in gene editing through the development of SyNTase™ editing platform.
Kraig Biocraft Laboratories technology is built on a scientifically engineered silkworm incorporating key spider silk proteins.
Intellia Therapeutics awarded inducement grants to six new employees under its 2024 Inducement Plan as a material inducement to employment.
Aurora Therapeutics is advancing a strategy to translate CRISPR gene editing into scalable, commercially viable medicines for rare diseases.
Regulatory discussions highlight the FDA increasing flexibility in cell and gene therapy development without lowering evidentiary standards.
Kraig Biocraft Laboratories is positioning its transgenic silkworm platform at the intersection of genetic engineering and textile manufacturing.
Kraig Biocraft Laboratories targets an output of up to 10 metric tons of recombinant spider-silk cocoons per month across three Vietnam facilities.
Recombinant spider silk fibers have achieved tensile strengths up to 1.79 gigapascals in testing with elasticity above 38%.
Kraig Biocraft Laboratories is initially targeting the apparel sector to leverage lower regulatory barriers and faster commercialization timelines.
Industry recognition includes Kraig’s spider silk work being featured on the cover of the March 2026 issue of National Geographic.
Aurora Therapeutics CEO Dr. Edward Kaye discussed the company’s strategy during a podcast episode focused on scaling CRISPR for rare disease.
The global DNA Read, Write and Edit market is projected to surge to $67.7 billion by 2030 driven by CRISPR advances and expanding clinical applications.
Kraig Biocraft Laboratories delivered over 1.3 metric tons of spider silk cocoons in a single month, representing a 5X increase over the previous high.
Rapid expansion in clinical applications and increased investment in CRISPR-based therapeutics are fueling demand for genomic diagnostics.
CRISPR Therapeutics has established strategic collaborations with leading biopharmaceutical partners including Vertex Pharmaceuticals.
CASGEVY is the world’s first CRISPR-based therapy approved for eligible patients with sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia.
Kraig Biocraft Laboratories successfully deployed more than 700,000 BAM-1 Alpha production hybrids of its previously reported 1,000,000 egg inventory.
The net proceeds from the convertible notes offering are estimated to be approximately $536.3 million for general corporate purposes.
Kraig Labs technology is built on a scientifically engineered silkworm which incorporates key spider silk proteins to produce recombinant spider silk.
BCC Research provides objective measurement and assessment of market opportunities with detailed market research reports through 2030.
Intellia Therapeutics operates as a leading clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on revolutionizing medicine leveraging CRISPR gene editing.
The company aims to transform the lives of people with severe diseases by developing and commercializing potentially curative treatments.
Intellia presented longer-term clinical data for Lonvoguran Ziclumeran for Hereditary Angioedema at AAAAI 2026.
Aurora Therapeutics is executing a strategy for translating CRISPR gene editing into scalable, commercially viable medicines for rare diseases.
Industry discussions highlight the FDA increasing flexibility in cell and gene therapy development without lowering evidentiary standards.
The podcast episode featuring Aurora Therapeutics' CEO Dr. Edward Kaye addresses scaling CRISPR technologies.
Intellia aims to reset the standard for medicine by durably treating the root causes of disease.
Intellia awarded inducement grants to six new employees to support its CRISPR-focused operations.
The inducement grants consisted of time-based restricted stock units for an aggregate of 16,500 shares of Intellia’s common stock.
Awards were approved by Intellia’s compensation committee as a material inducement to entering into employment.
Recent reporting highlights the ongoing development of exon-skipping therapies for Duchenne muscular dystrophy within the genetic medicine sector.
Sarepta Therapeutics has generated over $5.5 billion in revenue from drugs that may or may not provide significant benefit.
Exon-skipping drugs previously sparked a civil war within the Food and Drug Administration regarding approval standards.
A top FDA official previously overrode reviewers to approve the first of several exon-skipping candidates under pressure from advocates.
Clinical trial results for a new exon-skipping drug have stunned some of the field’s top experts.
Advocacy groups such as CureDuchenne have raised funds to support scientists launching startups around genetic strategies.
The strategy involves fixing a broken gene by breaking it just a little bit more through exon skipping.
Industry tags associate these rare disease developments with gene editing and gene therapy classifications.
Patient families remain central to the progression of rare disease genetic therapies and clinical trial enrollment.
Regulatory scrutiny remains high for genetic medicine candidates targeting rare diseases like Duchenne muscular dystrophy.
